title: Circuit Breakers for Tools
concept: Temporarily disabling a tool for all agents when it hits rate limits or experiences downstream outages.

exercises.prompt: What is an important strategy when designing systems to handle failures?
exercises.explain: Designing for potential failures helps create more resilient systems that can better handle unexpected problems.

cards.title: What Are Circuit Breakers?
cards.body: Circuit breakers protect systems by temporarily disabling tools when they encounter problems like rate limits or outages.

cards.title: Definition of Circuit Breakers
cards.body: Circuit breakers are safety mechanisms that stop tools from overworking when they face issues, ensuring the entire system stays stable.

cards.title: Analogy: Plumbing System
cards.body: Think of circuit breakers like a valve in plumbing. If there's too much pressure, the valve closes temporarily to prevent leaks or bursts.

cards.title: Example of a Circuit Breaker
cards.body: If a tool receives too many requests and hits a limit, it can trigger a circuit breaker to pause its operations until it's safe to continue.

cards.title: Myth: Circuit Breakers are Bad
cards.body: Some think circuit breakers slow everything down, but they actually protect the system and improve overall reliability!

cards.title: Visualizing Circuit Breakers
cards.body: Circuit breakers help manage system stress during issues.

exercises.prompt: What does a circuit breaker do during a problem?

options.text: Has no effect on the tool

exercises.explain: A circuit breaker temporarily disables the tool to protect the system from stress or failure until it's safe to operate again.

exercises.prompt: Circuit breakers are only used when the system is operating perfectly.
exercises.explain: Circuit breakers are used to protect the system during issues, not just when things are going perfectly.

exercises.prompt: A circuit breaker temporarily disables a tool when it hits __________ limits.
exercises.sentence: A circuit breaker temporarily disables a tool when it hits rate limits.
exercises.explain: Rate limits are important as they indicate when the tool needs a break to avoid problems.

exercises.prompt: Which choice best describes a circuit breaker in an AI tool?

options.text: A mechanism that disables a tool during outages
A report that shows tool performance

exercises.explain: A circuit breaker is specifically designed to safeguard the system by disabling tools during outages, ensuring reliability.

exercises.prompt: What happens when a tool reaches its rate limit?

options.text: The tool continues working without interruption
The circuit breaker pauses the tool's operations
The tool shuts down the entire system

exercises.explain: When a tool reaches its rate limit, the circuit breaker steps in to pause its operations, helping to protect the entire system from further issues.
